title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
description: Without proper authorization checks, users can potentially access resources or perform actions that they are not authorized to access by manipulating identifiers (e.g., database keys, file paths) directly in URLs or requests. This could allow a user to gain administrative privileges or access sensitive data.
attack_vector: Direct manipulation of identifiers in URLs or requests to access unauthorized resources.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization controls to verify user permissions before granting access to resources.
Use indirect object references (e.g., UUIDs) instead of direct identifiers.
Enforce the principle of least privilege – grant users only the minimum necessary permissions.
